zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Isabel Warren於1492年出生在英國爾波易頓時，她的父親Sir Laurence Warenne Lord of Poynton16歲，母親Lady Baroness Margaret de Legh22歲。她於1510年與John Rouse在英國英格蘭巴金哈姆爾哈登哈姆結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世於1546年在英國巴金哈姆爾哈登哈姆享年54歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭爾斯托普特。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret Isabel Warren于1492年出生在英国爾波易頓时，她的父亲Sir Laurence Warenne Lord of Poynton16岁，母亲Lady Baroness Margaret de Legh22岁。她于1510年与John Rouse在英国英格兰巴金哈姆爾哈登哈姆结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。她去世于1546年在英国巴金哈姆爾哈登哈姆享年54岁，埋葬在英国英格兰爾斯托普特。

nameMeanings.name: Warren
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from the Middle English (Old French) personal name Warin, Werin, a borrowing of ancient Germanic Warino, a short form of various compound names based on the element warin ‘protection, shelter’ or ‘guard’. Compare Waring .

English and Irish (of Norman origin): habitational name from La Varrenne in Seine-Maritime, France, named with a Gaulish element probably descriptive of alluvial land or sandy soil. This was the name of a major Norman family after the Conquest. In Ireland, this name has been Gaelicized as Bharain.
Irish: adopted as an English form of Gaelic Ó Murnáin (see Murnane , Warner ).

variants.name: Warrens
meanings: English: variant of Warren with genitival or excrescent -s. This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Warrener
meanings: English (of Norman origin): occupational name from Old French warrennier ‘warrener’, an officer employed to watch over game in a park or game preserve. Compare Warren .

variants.name: Murnane
meanings: Irish (Munster): Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Murnáin, a shortened and dissimilated form of Ó Manannáin ‘descendant of Manannán’, name borne in Celtic mythology by a sea god associated with the Isle of Man. All three syllables appear in the variant Marrinan. This name has also been Anglicized as Warren and Warner .

variants.name: Waren
meanings: English (Lancashire): variant of   Warren  .

variants.name: Warne
meanings: shortened form of   Warren  .
occasionally a habitational name from Wearne in Somerset. The place takes its name from a lost stream-name based on British verno- ‘alder, swamp’.
English:

variants.name: Waring
meanings: English (Lancashire and Yorkshire): variant of Warren , from the Norman personal name Warin, derived from ancient Germanic Warino, a short form of various compound names based on the element warin ‘protection, shelter’ or ‘guard’. The name was popular in France and among the Normans (see Guerin ).

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

nameMeanings.name: Isabel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabell
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. variants.name: Isabelle
meanings: form of   Isabel  , now in frequent use in the English-speaking world.

It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Isabel Warren was born in 1492, in Poynton, Cheshire, England, her father, Sir Laurence Warenne Lord of Poynton, was 16 and her mother, Lady Baroness Margaret de Legh, was 22. She married John Rouse in 1510, in Haddenham, Buckinghamshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died in 1546, in Haddenham, Buckinghamshire, England, at the age of 54, and was buried in Stockport, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret Isabel Warren est née en 1492, à Poynton, Cheshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir Laurence Warenne Lord of Poynton, avait 16 ans et sa mère, Lady Baroness Margaret de Legh, avait 22 ans. Elle a épousé John Rouse en 1510, à Haddenham, Buckinghamshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Elle est décédée en 1546, à Haddenham, Buckinghamshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 54 ans, et a été enterrée à Stockport, Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

Lei sposato John Rouse nel 1510, nel Haddenham, Buckinghamshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. ja.lifeSummary: Margaret Isabel Warrenが1492年にイギリスチェシュアイアポイントンで生まれた時，父Sir Laurence Warenne Lord of Poyntonは16歳，母Lady Baroness Margaret de Leghは22歳であった。1510年にイギリスイギリスブクインガムシュアイアハッドエンハムでJohn Rouseと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼女は1546年にイギリスブクインガムシュアイアハッドエンハムで54歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスチェシュアイアストクプオルトにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Isabel Warren(이)가 1492년 영국 체실르 포이턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Laurence Warenne Lord of Poynton(은)는 16세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lady Baroness Margaret de Legh(은)는 22세였다. 그녀는 1510년 영국 잉글랜드 벅잉함실르 하데함에서 John Rouse(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1546년 영국 벅잉함실르 하데함에서 54세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 스톡포트에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Isabel Warren nasceu em 1492, em Poynton, Cheshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Laurence Warenne Lord of Poynton, tinha 16 anos e sua mãe, Lady Baroness Margaret de Legh, tinha 22 anos. Ela casou-se com John Rouse em 1510, em Haddenham, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret Isabel Warren родилась в 1492 г. в местности поинтон, чешир, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir Laurence Warenne Lord of Poynton было 16 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Baroness Margaret de Legh, 22. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Rouse в 1510 г. в местности хадденам, буккингхамшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Она умерла в 1546 г. в местности хадденам, буккингхамшир, Англия в возрасте 54 лет и была похоронена в местности стоккпорт, чешир, Англия, Великобритания.

Se casó con John Rouse en 1510, en Haddenham, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió en 1546, en Haddenham, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 54 años, y fue sepultada en Stockport, Cheshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
